description: The PKM is a soviet/russian belt-fed, general-purpose machine gun. It's chambered in the ancient 7.62x54mmR cartridge, which is heavy and has a lot of kick, but each bullet will go through several zombies, and they come in 250-round belts.

In a controversial move, a toy company released this miniaturised version of the Bolter from Warhammer 40k as cosplay prop. It fires large nerf darts, which 'happen' to be exactly 40x46mm in size. Using loopholes and legal grey areas, they managed to sell it on the civilian market. It became wildly popular a few year before the cataclysm. It's still toy-grade quality, so don't expect precision or durability.

A minor private arsenal noted the success of the not-40mm bolter toy, and developed an actual militarised version of it. It's slightly heavier, but military-grade internals ensure good reliability and precision.
